title: Multi-horizon hazard targets instead of only next-lap labels
summary: Discrete-time survival work and recent F1 pit-stop research both suggest next-lap decisions benefit from jointly modeling time-to-event structure.
rationale: Discrete-time survival work and recent F1 pit-stop research both suggest next-lap decisions benefit from jointly modeling time-to-event structure. A single binary `PitNextLap` label discards whether the stop is 2-3 laps away.
implementation_hint: Create auxiliary targets such as `PitWithin2`, `PitWithin3`, or `LapsUntilNextPit` within each `Race_Year x Driver` sequence. Train separate models and blend, or a multitask model if the wrapper allows it. Enforce hazard consistency by deriving `P(next lap)` from cumulative horizons instead of treating each horizon independently.
expected_effect: Smoother decision boundary around pit windows and fewer false spikes on borderline laps.
risk: Auxiliary labels are future-derived and must be generated strictly within training folds. Poorly chosen horizons can just duplicate the base target.
